Ad Text:
Data Engineer - Or Lead Data Engineer 2 Roles.
(Databricks | Marketing Data & APIs / Financial Services)

$150K–$180K + Super | Baulkham Hills, Sydney (Hybrid)

🚀 Key Highlights

Databricks-focused role (AWS environment) – core to the platform

Work directly with Google Ads, Meta, GA & other Marketing APIs

Build end-to-end data pipelines (not just small components)

Blend of data engineering + MarTech (rare skillset)

Join a mature data environment with real ownership

Strong flexibility + excellent benefits

About the Role

We’re looking for a Data Engineer to build and scale a modern data platform that powers marketing analytics and customer insights.

You’ll integrate data from marketing platforms (Google Ads, Google Analytics, Meta APIs, etc.) with internal customer data to create a unified view of performance and behaviour across the business.

This is a hands-on role where you’ll own pipelines in Databricks, working closely with analysts and data scientists to deliver high-impact data solutions.

What You’ll Be Doing

Build and maintain data pipelines in Databricks (AWS)

Integrate and model data from marketing APIs (Google Ads, Meta, GA, etc.)

Develop scalable data models for analytics and reporting

Support data workflows and ML pipelines

Work closely with business stakeholders to deliver insights

Ensure data quality, performance, and reliability

What We’re Looking For

Must Have:

Strong experience with Databricks

Solid SQL + Python skills

Experience building end-to-end data pipelines

Exposure to marketing data / APIs (Google Ads, Meta, GA, etc.)

Experience working in cloud environments (AWS preferred)

Nice to Have:

Experience supporting ML/data science workflows

Experience with customer or behavioural data

Why Join?

Own real, end-to-end data engineering work

Work on high-impact marketing and customer data use cases

Be part of a team where you can take ownership and grow quickly

Strong benefits including flex working, allowances, and on-site perks

If you want a role where you’re not just maintaining pipelines—but actually building and shaping a data platform with real business impact, this is it.

Our leading Federal Government client are hiring for a Senior Cloud Infrastructure Automation Engineer to join the organisation. The Infrastructure Engineer will manage and maintain ICT platforms operating on RHEL Standard Operating Environment (SOE) Ecosystem, including all technical landscape design, installation, configuration, deployment, technical security and operation of ICT components and databases.

This role has a preference for candidates based in Canberra, however candidates in Adelaide, Brisbane, Melbourne and Sydney can be considered. This role requires an Negative Vetting Level 1 status (NV1) in order to start.


Start Day: 1st of July, 2026 - 12 Month Initial Contract

Role Title: Senior Cloud Infrastructure Automation Engineer

Daily Rate: $950 - $1,100 + Super per day.

Location: Canberra (2601) is the preferred location. Candidates from Brisbane (4000), Melbourne (3000), Sydney (2000) or Adelaide (5000) will also be considered.
The role is 3 days per week in office/WFH Hybrid.


Key Accountabilities:

Manage the automation, implementation and maintenance of Midrange Unix Infrastructure
Modernise the current IBM Power Private Cloud environment
Migrate the Network File System (NFS) from Solaris and retire Hitachi Network Attached Storage (HNAS)
Patch Solaris environments including upgrading SOE versions

Essential Criteria - Technical Skills:

Unix System Administration for Solaris
Linux System Administration for RHEL
Ansible Automation Platform
Red Hat OpenStack/OpenShift
IBM Power
Networking (configuration, operation and troubleshooting)
Scripting
Virtual Machine Provisioning
IBM Advanced Interactive eXecutive (AIX)

Candidates are required to be based in the above listed Capital Cities and need to be in office a minimum of 3 days per week. Additionally, you must possess Australian Citizenship and be in possession of a NV1 Security Clearance, or have the ability to obtain this clearance.


If you feel this opportunity matches your skills and previous experiences, please apply with your CV.

A new transformational adventure awaits you… 

How does 5 weeks of annual leave, a fulfilling career you'll genuinely enjoy, and working for a reputable organization that promotes wellbeing and drives digital transformation sound to you?

As one of Forbes 2024 ‘World’s Best Employers’ and Fortune’s ‘World’s Most Admired Companies’ for 2025, Zurich - a leader in the insurance industry, is on a journey towards a brighter future… A brighter future for you - focused on your career, your wellbeing, and your community - as well as our customers and the planet.

Join us on this thrilling journey as we work towards our vision of becoming Australia's leading digital insurer. If you're eager to be part of a values-driven global organisation that's experiencing real growth and transformation, and if you share our commitment to making a tangible difference and taking continuous steps towards a sustainable future for our people, customers, communities, and our planet, then keep reading!

Let’s create a brighter future together, let’s make a difference  

It’s important to know, what you do matters. At Zurich, we don’t just cover, we care.

As a Machine Learning Engineer (Agentic AI), you’ll play a key role in building and running LLM-powered applications and intelligent agents that improve how our customers and colleagues interact with Zurich. You’ll sit within our AI Platforms team, partnering closely with senior engineers, Tech Leads and product teams to turn emerging AI capability into practical, secure, and reliable solutions.

This role exists to help us responsibly scale agentic AI across the organisation. You’ll contribute to systems that are designed for real-world use, with a strong focus on operational readiness, quality engineering, and continuous improvement as we progress our technology transformation.

You will also be responsible for the following:

Build and maintain core components for agentic AI solutions, including orchestration flows, prompts, routing patterns, and structured outputs
Contribute to the delivery and productionisation of LLM and agent features in partnership with senior engineers and architects
Integrate AI solutions with internal services and data sources using APIs and basic resiliency patterns
Support LLMOps and MLOps automation across CI/CD, environments, and release practices
Implement practical guardrails such as grounding, validation, and permission-aware access
Embed quality engineering practices including testing, documentation, and secure coding

Important to your success – let’s grow together

You’re curious, pragmatic, and motivated by impact. You enjoy working collaboratively, balancing experimentation with responsibility, and you’re comfortable engaging with a broad range of stakeholders to turn ideas into outcomes.

In order to be successful, you will have the following:

Proven experience in AWS Cloud Engineering, designing and operating secure, scalable cloud-based services
Hands-on experience delivering ML or AI-enabled features into production environments
Strong engineering fundamentals using Python, APIs, and data-focused technologies
Experience with Infrastructure as Code and CI/CD pipelines in an enterprise setting
Exposure to LLM-based applications, including prompting and structured outputs
Experience working with containerisation and deployment automation

Belong. Zurich is here to support you 

There are so many reasons why Zurich is a great place to be and the right choice for you, but here are just a few.

Enjoy 5 weeks of annual leave for permanent staff through our Z leave initiative, along with 15 days of personal/carer’s leave as we empower our people to manage their own wellbeing. Plus, access to Family Care (parental leave).
Benefit from a hybrid working arrangement – 3 days in the office and 2 days working from home per week for full-time employees.
Access programs to stay healthy and feel healthier, receive an AUD$500 wellbeing payment per year, and a AUD$295 new starter e-voucher for your work-from-home setup (flex work - hybrid working), along with other great benefits through our Shine portal.
Grow and thrive in your career with our award-winning training programs. We support and encourage your development because our strength lies in our people, and we value your individuality.
Work with global, diverse, and inclusive teams; explore your passions, fuel innovation, drive equity, collaborate, and make a positive difference beyond your role.
Take advantage of global mobility opportunities across more than 200 countries worldwide.
Volunteer and work in our state-of-the-art Green Star rated offices (when you are in the office) and dress for your day.
We plant a tree for every new employee.
Not to mention our various employer of choice awards/memberships – WGEA; Family Inclusive Workplace; Gold AWEI Employer, Pride in Diversity… to name a few.

We could go on, but the main point is that Zurich is a great place to be, where you can truly belong, be yourself, maintain work/life balance, and thrive in a supportive environment.

Zurich is an equal opportunity employer.  We are committed to ensuring that our recruitment process is fair and accessible for all candidates. If you require any special accommodations to participate in our recruitment process, we encourage you to please let us know at the time of your application.  

So, if you want to come on an adventure that will build a brighter future together, and feel a sense of togetherness, then please apply today to start your adventure!

AI Engineer 
Contract: 12 Month Contract 
Location: Sydney or Melbourne

About the Role
We’re looking for an experienced AI Engineer to support a major uplift of the organisation’s data and analytics capability. This role will focus on enhancing the Power BI platform, improving data accessibility, and enabling deeper business insights. You’ll work closely with product, data, and operational teams to design and deliver AI-driven solutions that support smarter decision-making and automation across the organisation.
 
Key Responsibilities
Lead the uplift and optimisation of the Power BI platform, ensuring high-quality dashboards and reporting
Build and maintain AI and automation solutions that support operational and commercial decision-making
Work with large, complex datasets to surface insights on business spend, performance, and efficiency
Develop scalable data pipelines and models using Azure services
Design and implement solutions leveraging Azure SQL, SQL Server, and related data technologies
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business needs into technical solutions
Ensure data quality, governance, and best practice engineering standards
Support ongoing enhancements, experimentation, and continuous improvement across AI and analytics initiatives
Skills & Experience
Strong experience as an AI Engineer, Data Engineer, or similar role
Hands-on expertise with Azure (Data Factory, Synapse, Functions, Logic Apps, etc.)
Advanced SQL skills, including Azure SQL and relational database design
Proven experience in uplifting or modernising Power BI environments
Ability to work with complex datasets and build scalable data pipelines
Experience delivering AI, ML, or automation solutions in a commercial environment
Strong communication skills and ability to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Background in utilities, energy, or other complex operational environments is highly regarded
Why This Role
This is a chance to work on meaningful AI solutions that directly influence real-world operations at scale. You’ll be part of a growing AI and data capability, shaping how the organisation uses insights to drive performance and investment decisions.


We are an inclusive employer committed to fostering a diverse and accessible workplace. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disabilities, LGBTQIA+ individuals, people of all ages, and those from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

We are seeking an experienced Integration/Cloud Engineer to design, build, and maintain secure, scalable, cloud-native integration solutions to enable seamless data exchange across program systems.

This role is critical to ensuring interoperability within the Azure ecosystem and supporting reliable, high-performance data flows aligned to program delivery timelines.

Role Overview
Working closely with the Architecture Lead, Stream Lead, and delivery teams, the you will implement integration patterns, develop APIs, and orchestrate data pipelines. The role will also ensure integration services are secure, observable, and aligned with enterprise standards.

Key Responsibilities

Design and implement integration solutions using Azure Integration Services.
Develop and manage APIs, including lifecycle management, policy enforcement, and security.
Build and optimise ETL/ELT pipelines for data movement and transformation.
Apply integration patterns such as Publish/Subscribe, Request-Response, and Message Queuing.
Ensure secure integrations using OAuth2/JWT and enterprise security standards.
Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business and technical requirements into integration solutions.
Implement monitoring, logging, and alerting for integration services.
Support CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure automation (IaC).
Troubleshoot and resolve integration issues across environments.
Core Technical Skills

Azure Integration Services: API Management, Data Factory, Logic Apps, Service Bus, Event Grid, Functions
API Management: Policy definition, throttling, transformation, authentication
Data Integration: Strong experience with ADF, ETL/ELT pipelines, Azure SQL/Data Lake
DevOps & IaC: Azure DevOps/GitHub Actions, Terraform or Bicep
Development: C# (.NET), PowerShell, SQL, JSON, XML
The Must Haves:

5+ years in software/integration engineering
Minimum 3+ years hands-on Azure experience
Proven delivery of enterprise-grade integration solutions
Proven experience integrating CRM systems, finance platforms and enterprise applications using RESTful services and middleware tools.
Key Deliverables

Scalable and secure integration architecture components
API and data integration pipelines aligned to program milestones
Deployment pipelines and automated environments
Monitoring and support artefacts for ongoing operations
Success Measures

Stable, secure, and high-performing integration services
Timely delivery aligned with program schedule
Reduced integration defects and incidents
Maintainable and reusable integration components
Dependencies / Interfaces

Architecture and Cloud Engineering teams
Application and Data delivery streams  
You MUST be an Australian Citizen and hold a minimum of an NV1 Security Clearance to be considered for this role.
  
For more information contact Mitchell on 0425 269 780.
  
Only shortlisted candidate will be contacted for this role.

Our Story


4DMedical is a global medical technology company aiming to change the outcomes for patients with lung disease by revolutionizing respiratory imaging and ventilation analysis.

Our vision is a world in which people with lung disease have better health outcomes.

We are an ASX listed medical technology company and are currently in a high-growth stage of the business.

About the role

4DMedical is seeking a Machine Learning Engineer to join its Algorithm team.

The ideal candidate is a skilled Software Engineer with deep experience in training and deploying CNN-based deep learning models for image processing. The candidate should be able to demonstrate a strong approach to clean, reusable code and translate this to clean, reusable, training and inference code with traceable data governance.

You would be joining our team of Software Engineers as the first dedicated Machine Learning Engineer and be the specialist in developing new models in conjunction with our Research teams as well as maintaining, improving and monitoring our existing models


What you'll do

Design, develop and improve machine learning models.

Develop software to manage the training process of new and existing models.

Integrate the models into production code using Python and, occasionally, C++.

Design and implementation of production quality Medical Device software.

Collaborate with other teams to ensure design and implementation meet requirements.

Write or update design, verification and validation documents.


Qualifications

Master's degree in Computer Science or related fields.

Extensive experience in clean, well-structured Python.

Experience in machine learning techniques, across model design, training and inference.

Proven experience working in a highly test-driven environment.

Experience in developing on Linux with containerised images.

Demonstrated professional experience in Machine Learning, ideally backed with formal study or training courses

A real appreciation for the value of documentation in everything we do, using automated tooling where possible to demonstrate how and where data was used in every part of the model lifecycle.

A willingness to learn the Medical Device development lifecycle, with distinct stages of build, verify and then release.

Experience in highly-regulated industries such as medical and finance software will be helpful, but not essential in understanding how we work.



What can we offer you?

Hybrid working – when in the office enjoy our amazing new office space which includes end of trip facilities, and great local coffee and eateries!

Easy access to Public Transport and very close to Lygon St and the CBD

Frequent in-house presentations on all aspects of Lung Health & Technology.

Be a part of our Wellness program including free fruit, table tennis, free gym and corporate class pass, Employee Assistance Program for you and your family, free annual health, free skin check & free flu shot.

Rewards and Recognition Program including discounts for shopping and goods.

Laptop and accessories provided for remote work

Regular social events



This is an exceptional opportunity to join a forward-thinking, fast-growing company and make a real difference

How to Apply


Please apply via this link https://ats.rippling.com/en-GB/4dmedical/jobs/23f7957c-8a0a-4e94-b151-5dc5cac5ec57 DONT APPLY VIA SEEK
and submit a current CV and a cover letter which details your alignment with the requirements of the role.

4DMedical provides an inclusive work environment and embraces the diverse talent of its people.

The successful candidate will be required to undergo Police and Background checks.

For more company information visit our website: www.4dmedical.com

Should you have any queries regarding the position please contact the 4DMedical Recruitment Team at: recruitment@4dmedical.com

Job Title: Principal Data Platform Engineer (AI) - 12 Months Contract - AWS SageMaker
Ad Text:
Principal Data Platform Engineer (AI Platform) - 12 Months day rate Contract - AWS SageMaker

About the company
Our client is a major Australian enterprise undergoing significant AI-driven transformation. The organisation is scaling its data and AI capability, investing in modern platforms, and building future-ready foundations to enable secure, enterprise-wide AI innovation.

About the role
This Principal Data Platform Engineer role leads the hands-on build of a new AI and ML platform. Reporting to a Product Owner and working in a small cross-functional team, it drives platform engineering, architecture, delivery, and user enablement across the enterprise.

Benefits

Competitive daily rate
High-impact greenfield program
Opportunity to set enterprise AI platform standards
Flexible ways of working
Work with modern cloud-native technologies and emerging AI capability
Duties / Key responsibilities

Lead the build and operationalisation of the AI Workbench platform
Develop CI/CD, IaC, architecture artefacts, and scalable platform components
Provide technical leadership across cloud-native AI/ML services
Support user onboarding, platform adoption, optimisation, and performance
Mentor engineers and collaborate across architecture, security, and product teams
Skills and experience

10+ years delivering large-scale data and AI/ML platforms
Expertise across AWS (SageMaker, Bedrock, S3, IAM) and cloud-native engineering
Strong skills in Terraform/CloudFormation and CI/CD
Experience establishing platform engineering standards in greenfield environments
Ability to translate architecture into actionable technical design
Strong collaboration, communication, and problem-solving skills
Familiarity with LLMs, GenAI tooling, and prompt engineering (desirable)
AWS or DevOps/ML Ops certifications (desirable)
If you’re interested in this opportunity and feel you’re the right fit please click “apply” 

About Allura Partners
Allura Partners was founded to support private equity firms by placing C-Suites to lead, transform, and scale portfolio companies. Today, in addition to our proven private equity specialisation, we’ve become a trusted partner for major ASX-listed and private enterprises in Australia, offering executive search, recruitment, and business transformation. Known for integrity, reliability, and results, our expertise spans Executive Search, Accounting & Finance, Transformation & Change, and Technology & Digital. We connect businesses and top-tier talent, exceeding expectations and driving lasting impact.

Principal Data Platform Engineer (AI Platform) - 12 Months day rate Contract - AWS SageMaker

We currently have an exciting new opportunity for a dynamic and experienced Digital Engineer to join our Planning, Infrastructure & Engineering (PIE) team.

Explore how we are thinking big with exciting projects like the Third Runway, state-of-the-art terminals, and thriving business park developments. You can also learn more about life at Melbourne Airport – our culture, values, and what makes us unique at https://www.melbourneairport.com.au/corporate/our-culture-and-values. Join us in shaping the future of aviation as we propel Melbourne Airport towards becoming Australia's favourite Airport destination.

Key responsibilities:

The role's duties and accountabilities include, but are not limited to:

Manage and govern digital engineering data across capital projects, ensuring alignment with APAC standards
Conduct model audits, data validation and support model federation into Melbourne Airport’s master digital model
Support end-to-end digital information management from project planning through to operations
Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to respond to digital data requests and improve data quality
Act as a Digital Engineering SME, supporting systems such as ACC (BIM360), Revit, GIS and SharePoint
Contribute to the development of Melbourne Airport’s digital twin and continuous improvement initiatives 

The successful candidate will have:

Experience working within digital engineering or infrastructure project environments (2–5 years desirable)
Knowledge of digital design platforms such as ACC (BIM360), Revit, Revizto and SharePoint
Strong understanding of BIM processes and digital information management (ISO19650 knowledge advantageous)
Relevant qualifications in Digital Engineering, Civil, Structural, Services Engineering or Architecture
Strong communication skills with the ability to engage stakeholders across all levels
A proactive, solutions-focused mindset with a passion for innovation and continuous improvement 

We understand that not everyone will feel they meet all of these 100%. However, if you feel you have the attitude and aptitude to grow within our team, we invite you to apply and have a conversation with us about this position.

About the Company:

Melbourne Airport (Australia Pacific Airports Corporation) is proud to be one of Australia's premier transport hubs and Victoria's gateway to the world, connecting us to the global marketplace. We are committed to creating connections that matter and enhancing the traveller experience across every aspect of our organisation to deliver our vision of becoming Australia's favourite Airport destination. Currently, in an exciting phase of change, we are delivering rewarding career opportunities, making Melbourne Airport a dynamic and fast-paced place to work for our people.

APAC is a majority Australian-owned private company that also operates Launceston Airport, the tourism gateway to Tasmania and an industry multi-award-winning airport.

Culture and Values:

By joining the team at Melbourne Airport, you will work within a friendly, high-performance culture with a focus on our people and their development.

Our vision is to become Australia's favourite Airport destination and our people are paramount to achieving this success. We provide a supportive environment, with values that encourage all of our employees to Be You, Think Big, Own it and Work Together.

Benefits:

Free access to Higher State, our state-of-the-art Fitness Club & Wellness Hub at Novotel & ibis Styles Airport hotel.
Airport car parking 24 hours/365 days a year for both business and personal use
Hybrid flexible working arrangements
Airport Holiday, an additional day of leave
Wellness Day, a further additional day of leave
Discounted onsite childcare at Creative Garden Early Learning
Mental health and well-being initiatives, including Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Long-term career and professional development opportunities
Active social culture and participation in a range of social impact days and activities
Signature Experiences including an airfield family day
Access to an employee benefits scheme including access to corporate rates and airport retailer discounts (including SkyBus)

APAC is committed to weaving Diversity into our DNA. We believe that Diversity and Inclusion will help us realise our greatest potential as a company, and maximise the potential of all our team members. We strive to create a culture where our team members understand that each individual is unique and that embracing diversity makes us more flexible, productive, and creative.

APAC provides equal opportunity and advancement in employment regardless of gender, age, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, cultural background, caring responsibilities, disability or socioeconomic background. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds who share our workplace values of Being You, Thinking Big, Owning It and Working Together.

Better and fairer care. Always.

St Vincent's has been a leader in Australia's health and aged care landscape for more than 165 years. Since our founders, the Sisters of Charity, opened our first hospital in 1857, our services and nearly 30,000 people have been behind some of Australia's most important medical breakthroughs. We are a microcosm of Australia's health and aged care system and are uniquely positioned to lead and respond to our rapidly changing environment.

Are you an experienced Data Engineering leader ready to shape the future of healthcare technology? Join St Vincent's Health Australia (SVHA) and lead our transformation journey as we build an integrated Data, AI & Interoperability Platform to modernise healthcare delivery. This is your opportunity to pioneer the next generation of healthcare data infrastructure.

The Opportunity

St Vincent's Health Australia (SVHA) is launching its future as a "Connected, Intelligent Healthcare organisation" to unify data, AI and intelligence across our national footprint. As the Data Engineering & Innovation Lead, you will be the platform owner for our modern data and AI platform, guiding St Vincent's through an exciting transformation to cloud & AI-native data capabilities.

This is a unique opportunity to architect and lead the engineering function as we embark on our journey toward real-time IoT data integration, Generative AI and Machine Learning, self-service analytics, and data democratisation across our healthcare ecosystem.

You and your team will be responsible for bring structured and unstructured data from multiple sources at many speeds, transforming it into meaningful inputs for advanced analytics and AI. You will also be leading the establishing and growth of cutting edge AI capabilities as St Vincent's navigates its journey to increasingly embedding AI into the services it provides to its patients and residents.

Working closely with diverse stakeholders across clinical, operational and corporate functions, you'll build strong partnerships and implement process improvements that unlock the power of data to enhance patient care and operational excellence.

This role is part of a broader Data Analytics Community across SVHA, and all activities must align with SVHA's Data Governance & Analytics Strategy and Standards, ensuring we build a sustainable, scalable platform for the future.

What you will be doing.

Key Responsibilities

As Data Engineering & Innovation Lead, you will be expected to:

Serve as the technical platform owner for our modern Data & AI platform. Lead the design, implementation and evolution of data engineering solutions that enable real-time data processing, IoT integration, and advanced analytics capabilities across the organisation.
Drive innovation through Generative AI and Machine Learning capabilities, establishing frameworks and pipelines that enable predictive analytics, intelligent automation and data-driven decision making to transform patient care and operational efficiency.
Enable data democratisation by building robust, user-friendly data pipelines and self-service analytics capabilities that empower stakeholders across the organisation to access and leverage data insights independently and securely.
Design and implement cloud-native data architectures including data lakes, lakehouses, streaming platforms and interoperability layers. Establish data modelling standards, ETL/ELT processes and data quality frameworks that support scalable, reliable data operations.
Build strong relationships with clinical, operational and technical stakeholders to understand data needs, gather requirements and implement process change management. Act as a trusted advisor on data engineering best practices and innovation opportunities.
Ensure the Mission, Vision, Creed, and Values of SVHA (Compassion, Justice, Integrity, Excellence) are effectively integrated into all areas of responsibility, maintaining ethical data practices and patient-centered technology solutions.

What You'll Bring.

Essential Experience & Skills:

Tertiary qualification Degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field
Extensive experience in data engineering, data architecture, and modern cloud-native data platforms (Azure preferred, Fabric, databricks, snowflake desirable)
Proven track record of leading data engineering initiatives and serving as a platform owner for enterprise data solutions
Strong expertise in data modelling, ETL/ELT pipelines, data warehousing, and real-time data processing
Experience with AI/ML platforms, including Generative AI integration and MLOps practices
Demonstrated ability to design and implement self-service analytics and data democratisation initiatives
Deep understanding of data governance, data quality frameworks, and interoperability standards (particularly in healthcare)
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with ability to translate technical concepts for diverse audiences
Experience driving innovation and continuous improvement in complex organisational environments

Desirable:

Data, infrastructure and/or AI certifications by recognised providers (Microsoft, , Databricks, Snowflake etc.)
Knowledge and experience of the health industry and healthcare data standards (HL7, FHIR)
Experience with IoT data platforms and real-time streaming technologies
Track record of leading digital transformation initiatives in large, complex organisations

If you are driven to achieve results, possess strong technical leadership and innovation skills, and want to contribute to a workplace that is safe and promotes personal wellbeing while transforming healthcare through data and technology, we encourage you to apply.

Things that work for you:

Opportunity to lead the engineering transformation for a major healthcare organisation
Ability to visibly see your impact on patient care and health service delivery
Work with cutting-edge technologies including AI, ML, IoT and modern cloud platforms
Competitive salary packaging options
Discounted private health insurance
Employee Assistance Program for staff and their families
Access to the Fitness Passport (FP)

At St Vincent's we value inclusivity and diversity and acknowledge the importance of creating a work environment that is welcoming, safe, fair and impartial and invites people from all backgrounds to join us.

Pre-Employment Checks

Your employment is conditional upon the completion of all required pre-employment checks, including relevant immunisations.

Equal Opportunity

We celebrate diversity & inclusion and are committed to equal employment opportunity for everyone.

At St Vincent's we value inclusivity and diversity and acknowledge the importance of creating a work environment that is welcoming, safe, fair and impartial and invites people from all backgrounds to join us.

Pre-Employment Checks

Your employment is conditional upon the completion of all required pre-employment checks, including relevant immunisations.

Equal Opportunity

We celebrate diversity & inclusion and are committed to equal employment opportunity for everyone.

Better and fairer care. Always.

St Vincent's has been a leader in Australia's health and aged care landscape for more than 165 years. Since our founders, the Sisters of Charity, opened our first hospital in 1857, our services and nearly 30,000 people have been behind some of Australia's most important medical breakthroughs. We are a microcosm of Australia's health and aged care system and are uniquely positioned to lead and respond to our rapidly changing environment.

Are you an experienced Data Engineering leader ready to shape the future of healthcare technology? Join St Vincent's Health Australia (SVHA) and lead our transformation journey as we build an integrated Data, AI & Interoperability Platform to modernise healthcare delivery. This is your opportunity to pioneer the next generation of healthcare data infrastructure.

The Opportunity

St Vincent's Health Australia (SVHA) is launching its future as a "Connected, Intelligent Healthcare organisation" to unify data, AI and intelligence across our national footprint. As the Data Engineering & Innovation Lead, you will be the platform owner for our modern data and AI platform, guiding St Vincent's through an exciting transformation to cloud & AI-native data capabilities.

This is a unique opportunity to architect and lead the engineering function as we embark on our journey toward real-time IoT data integration, Generative AI and Machine Learning, self-service analytics, and data democratisation across our healthcare ecosystem.

You and your team will be responsible for bring structured and unstructured data from multiple sources at many speeds, transforming it into meaningful inputs for advanced analytics and AI. You will also be leading the establishing and growth of cutting edge AI capabilities as St Vincent's navigates its journey to increasingly embedding AI into the services it provides to its patients and residents.

Working closely with diverse stakeholders across clinical, operational and corporate functions, you'll build strong partnerships and implement process improvements that unlock the power of data to enhance patient care and operational excellence.

This role is part of a broader Data Analytics Community across SVHA, and all activities must align with SVHA's Data Governance & Analytics Strategy and Standards, ensuring we build a sustainable, scalable platform for the future.

What you will be doing.

Key Responsibilities

As Data Engineering & Innovation Lead, you will be expected to:


Serve as the technical platform owner for our modern Data & AI platform. Lead the design, implementation and evolution of data engineering solutions that enable real-time data processing, IoT integration, and advanced analytics capabilities across the organisation.
Drive innovation through Generative AI and Machine Learning capabilities, establishing frameworks and pipelines that enable predictive analytics, intelligent automation and data-driven decision making to transform patient care and operational efficiency.
Enable data democratisation by building robust, user-friendly data pipelines and self-service analytics capabilities that empower stakeholders across the organisation to access and leverage data insights independently and securely.
Design and implement cloud-native data architectures including data lakes, lakehouses, streaming platforms and interoperability layers. Establish data modelling standards, ETL/ELT processes and data quality frameworks that support scalable, reliable data operations.
Build strong relationships with clinical, operational and technical stakeholders to understand data needs, gather requirements and implement process change management. Act as a trusted advisor on data engineering best practices and innovation opportunities.
Ensure the Mission, Vision, Creed, and Values of SVHA (Compassion, Justice, Integrity, Excellence) are effectively integrated into all areas of responsibility, maintaining ethical data practices and patient-centered technology solutions.

What You'll Bring

Essential Experience & Skills:


Tertiary qualification Degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field
Extensive experience in data engineering, data architecture, and modern cloud-native data platforms (Azure preferred, Fabric, databricks, snowflake desirable)
Proven track record of leading data engineering initiatives and serving as a platform owner for enterprise data solutions
Strong expertise in data modelling, ETL/ELT pipelines, data warehousing, and real-time data processing
Experience with AI/ML platforms, including Generative AI integration and MLOps practices
Demonstrated ability to design and implement self-service analytics and data democratisation initiatives
Deep understanding of data governance, data quality frameworks, and interoperability standards (particularly in healthcare)
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with ability to translate technical concepts for diverse audiences
Experience driving innovation and continuous improvement in complex organisational environments

Desirable:

Data, infrastructure and/or AI certifications by recognised providers (Microsoft, , Databricks, Snowflake etc.)
Knowledge and experience of the health industry and healthcare data standards (HL7, FHIR)
Experience with IoT data platforms and real-time streaming technologies
Track record of leading digital transformation initiatives in large, complex organisations

If you are driven to achieve results, possess strong technical leadership and innovation skills, and want to contribute to a workplace that is safe and promotes personal wellbeing while transforming healthcare through data and technology, we encourage you to apply.

Things that work for you:


Opportunity to lead the engineering transformation for a major healthcare organisation
Ability to visibly see your impact on patient care and health service delivery
Work with cutting-edge technologies including AI, ML, IoT and modern cloud platforms
Competitive salary packaging options
Discounted private health insurance

Ad Text:
Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

The ServiceNow AI Engineer is responsible for designing, configuring and optimising Now Assist AI features (e.g., case summarisation, agent assistance and knowledge solutions) and builds high -quality prompts, instructions and model configurations using organisational data. The role implements and monitors AI safety controls (e.g., guardrails, filtering and compliance checks), integrates ServiceNow with external systems, and analyses performance to reduce hallucinations and improve accuracy.

What You'll Do

Deliver high -quality, reliable Now Assist experiences that are correctly configured, securely deployed, and adopted effectively by end users to create measurable value

Complete reliable integrations with external systems and data pipelines prepared for AI use

Analyse and improve Model performance baselined; hallucination rates reduced; outputs optimised from feedback

Use analytical and problem-solving evaluation frameworks and data to translate business challenges into AI solutions and improve AI quality

Escalate risks appropriately; make pragmatic trade-offs; conceptualise and develop innovative ideas, and take them from inception to production

What makes you perfect for the role?

Escalate risks appropriately; make pragmatic trade -offs ; conceptuali se and develop innovative ideas

Proven experience independently designing, configuring and optimising ServiceNow Now Assist or comparable generative/agentic AI solutions

Demonstrated, hands -on experience delivering AI capabilities in production environments, with clear accountability for outcomes

Strong communication and influencing skills, with the ability to communicate clearly and confidently with stakeholders

Ability to clearly explain AI behaviour, risks and decisions to non -technical stakeholders

What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.

Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.

Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.

Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.

We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.

Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.

Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).

Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

Free Optus bus from Macquarie University Metro Station (every 6-8 minutes); morning and afternoon.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170.

Ad Text:
Job No.: 692887

Location: Clayton campus

Employment Type: Full-time 

Duration: Fixed-term appointment until 1 June 2029

Remuneration: $124,343 - $137,251 pa HEW Level 08 (plus 17% employer superannuation), although a competitive remuneration package can be applied for an experienced candidate

Amplify your impact at a world top 50 University
Join our inclusive, collaborative community
Be surrounded by extraordinary ideas - and the people who discover them

The Opportunity

Monash University MAVERIC is a next-generation, high-performance compute platform and AI supercomputer that will support data-intensive and latency-sensitive workloads. We are seeking an experienced and proactive Senior Systems Engineer to lead the management and optimisation of our enterprise-grade, on-premises Kubernetes platform. This role will be responsible for engineering and maintaining a secure, automated, and highly observable platform and will be pivotal in enabling teams to run workloads that leverage bleeding-edge technology to advance global research.

You will be responsible for the full lifecycle management of Kubernetes platforms, operating at scale and high-performance environments, including:

Manage large-scale, production-grade on-premises Kubernetes platforms, operating across multiple clusters with a focus on ensuring security, performance, high availability, scalability, and resilience.
Deep understanding and implementation of advanced Kubernetes networking architectures, utilising CNI plugins, ingress controllers, and service meshes.
Operating a GitOps-driven platform, enabling fully declarative, version-controlled infrastructure and software delivery.
Developing and maintaining robust CI/CD pipelines using modern DevOps toolchains
Engineering secure platforms, implementing controls such as RBAC, runtime security controls, logging, workload isolation, and network policies.Managing and integrating high-performance storage solutions and virtualisation platforms to support resource-intensive workloads running concurrently.
Establish observability and platform reliability, implementing monitoring, logging, alerting, and applying SRE principles
Collaborating with cross-functional teams, including developers, security professionals, and researchers, to translate requirements into effective technical solutions.
Ensuring compliance with Monash University's technical, operational, and security standards across all systems.

To excel in this role, you should possess:

A degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
Extensive experience in designing and administering on-premises Kubernetes platforms, with a strong grasp of control plane components and its cluster internals.
Proficiency in Kubernetes networking and security, including hands-on experience with CNI plugins, ingress controllers, RBAC, runtime security controls, and network policies.
Experience with high-performance storage systems, virtualisation technologies, and data centre infrastructure.
Strong skills in Git, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure automation tools such as ArgoCD, Ansible, Azure DevOps, and Github.
Solid foundation in Linux systems, distributed systems and high-performance computing concepts, and infrastructure platform fundamentals
Excellent problem-solving abilities and effective communication skills, with the capacity to work collaboratively across diverse teams.

About Monash University

At Monash, work feels different. There’s a sense of belonging, from contributing to something ground breaking – a place where great things happen.

We value difference and diversity, and welcome and celebrate everyone's contributions, lived experience and expertise. That’s why we champion an inclusive and respectful workplace culture where everyone is supported to succeed.

Some 20,000 staff work for Monash around the world. We have 95,000 students, four Australian campuses, and campuses in Malaysia and Indonesia. We also have a major presence in India and China, and a significant centre and research foundation in Italy.

In our short history, we have skyrocketed through global university rankings and established ourselves consistently among the world's best tertiary institutions. We rank in the world’s top-50 universities in rankings including the QS World University Rankings 2026.

Learn more about Monash.

Today, we have the momentum to create the future we need for generations to come. Accelerate your change here. 

Monash supports flexible and hybrid working arrangements. We have a range of policies in place enabling staff to combine work and personal commitments. This includes supporting parents.

To Apply

For instructions on how to apply, please refer to 'How to apply for Monash Jobs'.

Diversity is one of our greatest strengths at Monash. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, culturally and linguistically diverse people, people with disabilities, neurodivergent people, and people of all genders, sexualities, and age groups.

We are committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible recruitment process at Monash. If you need any reasonable adjustments, please contact us at hr-recruitment@monash.edu in an email titled 'Reasonable Adjustments Request' for a confidential discussion.

Your employment is contingent upon the satisfactory completion of all pre-employment and/or background checks required for the role, as determined by the University.

Enquiries: Joseph Pineda, Infrastructure Delivery Lead, joseph.pineda@monash.edu 

Position Description: Senior Systems Engineer

Applications Close: Monday 4 May 2026, 11:55pm AEST

About Us

We are experts in board, CEO and C-Suite leadership with over 30 years of experience setting the gold standard in top-tier leadership development. Now, we’re looking to help leaders of all levels by automating, scaling, and augmenting our existing assessment and coaching processes. We move fast but we don't compromise quality. We need an engineer who can do both.

The Role

This is a founding engineering position. You will own the technical implementation of our product: building and maintaining the full stack, managing AI-assisted development workflows, and setting the engineering standards that the rest of the team will grow into.

You will not be doing this alone. A product-requirements document and MVP plan will be delivered to you by a senior technical consultant before you begin. Your job is to take this plan and execute it at high quality, with modern tooling, and with enough rigor that junior developers can contribute safely within the same system.

This role is for someone who has done the big-company thing and knows exactly what they'd do differently if given the chance.

What You'll Own

The full stack. Python backend, APIs, and user-facing applications. It will need to be architected cleanly and built to last.

AI-assisted development. You'll use agentic coding tools (Claude Code, Vibe, or equivalent) not as a novelty, but as a core part of how you work. We expect you to be fluent in this and to help define how our team uses these tools responsibly.

Code quality and standards. You'll establish the review processes, testing practices, and documentation norms that keep the codebase maintainable as the team grows.

Infrastructure and delivery. Cloud infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment, all owned end to end.

Junior developer oversight. As we grow, you'll bring on and guide junior contributors. The systems and standards you build now need to support that.

Who You Are

You have significant experience building and shipping production-grade systems — not just features, but entire stacks.

You're genuinely fluent with AI-assisted development. You've used LLM-based coding tools in your workflow and you have opinions about how to do it well.

You're comfortable with Python and cloud infrastructure. You write clean, testable, well-documented code as a matter of professional habit, not obligation.

You understand how to work from a specification. You don't need to invent the product, but you need to build it excellently.

You know how to move fast without creating chaos. You've lived through what happens when speed outpaces quality, and you've learned from it.

You're drawn to hard, meaningful problems. AI applied to human development and organizational performance is genuinely novel territory, and that excites you.




Compensation & Benefits

Option A — Market Rate (No Equity)

Competitive market-rate salary commensurate with experience

Full benefits package

Sydney, NSW in-person role with a team that respects your time and expertise

Option B — Below Market Rate + Equity

Salary below market rate, offset by meaningful equity in a company at an early, high-leverage stage

Full benefits package

Founding team-level ownership stake with real upside as we scale.

How to Apply

Send us your resume, a GitHub profile or project you're proud of, and short answers to the following:

Describe a time you balanced speed and quality under real pressure. What did you protect, and what did you let slide?

Tell us about a meaningful experience using AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor, or similar) to write or manage code. What worked, what didn't, and what would you do differently?

How would you approach building the MVP of an AI-powered executive coaching platform? Where would you start, and what would you be most careful about?

Job Title: Data Engineer-Medicare Mental Health Check In
Ad Text:
Build at scale with real impact - Design and optimise data platforms that directly support clinical care, operational performance, and national health initiatives
Work at the forefront of AI and modern data platforms - Contribute to data pipelines supporting Machine Learning, Generative AI, and advanced analytics use cases
Shape the future of data at enterprise level - Influence architecture, governance, and how data is leveraged across one of Australia's largest health systems

What if your next role didn't just build pipelines - but powered the future of healthcare in Australia?

At St Vincent's Health Australia, data is more than infrastructure - it is the foundation for better patient outcomes, smarter decisions, and system-wide transformation.

We're looking for a Data Engineer who wants to step into a role where their work underpins advanced analytics, AI, and next-generation digital health services across a national organisation.

About the Program - Medicare Mental Health Check-In
As part of this opportunity, you will contribute to the Medicare Mental Health Check-In - a national digital mental health service delivered in partnership with the Australian Government.

This program brings together national partners across technology, clinical workforce, and digital innovation to deliver a new model of accessible, scalable mental health care.

Data engineering is critical to enabling this model - ensuring high-quality, secure, and scalable data pipelines that support real-time insights, performance monitoring, and AI-driven innovation.

About St Vincent's Health Australia
St Vincent's Health Australia is one of Australia's leading not-for-profit health and aged care providers, founded by the Sisters of Charity in 1857.

Today, our 30,000-strong workforce delivers care across public hospitals, private hospitals, aged care, home care and virtual care services across NSW, Victoria and Queensland.

Our vision is to deliver excellent and compassionate care within a better and fairer health and aged care system.


About Your New Role
As a Data Engineer, you will sit at the core of our data ecosystem - designing, building, and optimising data pipelines that enable analytics, reporting, and emerging AI capabilities.

You will work across clinical, operational, and technology teams to ensure data is accessible, reliable, and structured to support decision-making at scale.

This role goes beyond traditional data engineering - you will contribute to modern data platforms, AI readiness, and enterprise-wide data capability uplift.

This is a 12 month fixed term ,full-time role based at our Melbourne Group Office (hybrid), with national impact.

In this role , you will :


Design, build and optimise scalable data pipelines (ETL/ELT) across modern data platforms
Develop and maintain data warehouse and lakehouse environments (Azure, Databricks, Fabric)
Implement software engineering best practices (CI/CD, testing, version control)
Perform data modelling, architecture design, and optimisation
Enable AI and ML readiness through high-quality, governed data pipelines
Support development of vector databases and pipelines for Generative AI use cases (e.g. RAG)
Ensure data security, governance, and compliance with healthcare standards
Collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and business stakeholders
Monitor, troubleshoot, and continuously improve data platforms and pipelines
Contribute to documentation, standards, and uplift of data engineering capability

About You
You are a highly capable Data Engineer who thrives in complex environments and enjoys building robust, scalable data solutions.

You're confident working across technical and business teams, and bring a strong focus on quality, performance, and long-term data architecture.

Essential Experience


5+ years' experience in data engineering, data warehousing, or data architecture
Strong experience with Azure data platforms (ADF, Databricks, Fabric)
Proficiency in Python or Scala, alongside advanced SQL
Experience designing ETL/ELT pipelines and modern data architectures
Strong understanding of data modelling, relational databases, and warehouse design
Experience with Power BI, SSAS, or similar analytics tools
Knowledge of CI/CD, DevOps practices, and Infrastructure as Code

Desirable


Experience in healthcare or regulated environments
Exposure to Machine Learning, MLOps, or Generative AI (RAG, LLMs)
Experience with containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes)
Understanding of structured and unstructured data environments

Why Join St Vincent's?
This is more than a technical role - it's an opportunity to build the data foundations that power better care.

At St Vincent's, you will:


Work on meaningful, high-impact data challenges
Be part of a purpose-driven organisation grounded in Compassion, Integrity, Justice and Excellence
Access salary packaging benefits to increase your take-home pay
Work in a collaborative, forward-thinking data and technology environment
Contribute to large-scale digital health and AI transformation initiatives

Ready to Apply?
If you're passionate about building data platforms that make a real difference, we'd love to hear from you.

Please apply with:


An up-to-date CV
A tailored cover letter
Responses to the mandatory application questions below

Mandatory Application Questions
(Maximum 500 words per question)

1. Data Engineering at Scale
Describe your experience designing and building data pipelines or data platforms in a complex environment. What technologies did you use and what impact did your work have?

2. Data Quality, Performance and Optimisation
Provide an example where you improved data quality, performance, or reliability within a data platform. What approach did you take?

3. Stakeholder Collaboration
Tell us about a time you worked with both technical and non-technical stakeholders to deliver a data solution. How did you ensure alignment and successful delivery?

Please note: Applications are reviewed as they are received. The role may close early if a suitable candidate is identified.

Important Information
Your employment is subject to pre-employment checks, including relevant immunisations.

Equal Opportunity
We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and abilities - including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, culturally and linguistically diverse communities, LGBTQIA+ communities and people with lived experience of mental health challenges. Inclusion is essential to our mission - and diversity reflects the community we serve.

At BildGroup, we’re proud to be recognised as a trusted name in the civil infrastructure industry, delivering high-quality construction projects across Australia. We’re looking for an Integration Engineer to join our IT team and play a critical role in connecting the systems that run BildGroup.

Bank First was born by making a BOLD move. Now, we're on a mission to make more BOLD moves as we transform and grow.

We are proud to provide our community of educators and health-care workers with a bank that puts them first, because they put others first. We're committed to building a unique bank - one that’s different, sustainable and centred around our Members.

Join us in breaking the mould and shaping the future of banking. We’re looking for innovative and creative minds that are ready to make a real impact at a place where you can truly be yourself.

So, what’s the role?

The Domain Architect leads Bank First’s AI and data architecture, setting direction, standards and target designs while staying hands-on with delivery. The role ensures alignment with data governance (including quality, lineage, classification and responsible AI) and translates business needs into secure, practical architectures for engineering and delivery teams. 

In a typical day you will get involved in:

Own and evolve AI & data architecture (principles, standards, controls) aligned to strategy and risk
Define target-state architecture and roadmap to modern data/AI capability
Develop reusable reference architectures and patterns
Design secure, scalable AI solutions (including GenAI and Agentic AI where appropriate)
Evaluate AI features in core and SaaS platforms (integration, data, security, governance)
Translate business problems into clear architectural requirements and options
Lead solution architecture for key initiatives from design through delivery
Communicate with stakeholders and align governance standards into practical guardrails

About you

You balance big-picture thinking with a practical approach to delivery and you’re comfortable working in a small-team environment. You’re curious and evidence-led, testing assumptions and using data to guide decisions and iteration.

You are comfortable navigating ambiguity, setting direction and making decisions with imperfect information. 

Ideally, you’ll have:

Proven experience in data and AI/ML architecture within complex (ideally regulated) environments
Strong expertise in modern data platforms (modelling, integration, lineage, quality, semantic layers)
Solid knowledge  and experience of Azure data and AI services and fit-for-purpose architecture patterns
Hands-on experience with Generative and Agentic AI solutions (e.g., RAG, prompt design, orchestrations, safe deployments ,etc)
Ability to produce high-quality architecture artefacts and transition plans
Strong stakeholder management, communication and influencing skills
10+ years’ architecture experience with TOGAF (or similar) principles and hands-on delivery collaboration

Why join us?

For-purpose, a bank committed to helping its Members and their community.
Impactful work that will genuinely make a difference to the lives of our Members!
Work with values-driven leaders, who actively develop talent and support you to grow your career. In an environment that you are empowered to Be Bold. Be Different. Be Sustainable. Be YOU!
Work-life balance and flexibility, so you can work in a way that best suits you and supports your commitments outside of work. Family friendly parental leave, and paid superannuation up to 52 weeks.
Hybrid working from our state-of-the-art offices to connect and collaborate, as well as focused time at home.
Competitive remuneration with an additional one week ‘Be You’ leave, ‘Be Well’ wellbeing benefit, #BFF Club home loan offer and free on-site parking!

Are you ready to be part of something bigger? At Lite n' Easy, we are not just about meals; we are about transforming lives through better eating habits. We make it effortless for Aussies to embrace healthier choices, whether they are on a weight loss journey or simply aiming to maintain a balanced lifestyle. As a team, we pour our passion into crafting meals that are not only nutritious but also bursting with flavour.




The Role

Reporting to the ICT Manager, the Cloud Engineer is a pivotal role dedicated to designing, automating, and scaling our digital infrastructure. You won’t just be managing servers; you will be the architect of our cloud first future. Your mission is to leverage Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and CI/CD to ensure our systems are resilient, secure, and ready for growth. Whether it’s optimizing our Azure environment or managing cross platform connectivity with AWS, you will be the technical lead driving our digital evolution.

The Skills You Bring

To be successful in this role, you’ll need a "code first" mindset and a deep understanding of modern cloud ecosystems:

Cloud Expertise: Proven experience in cloud engineering with a primary focus on Azure, supported by working knowledge of AWS.
Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Advanced proficiency in Bicep, ARM templates, or CloudFormation. You believe that manual configuration is a last resort.
DevOps & Automation: Hands on experience with Azure DevOps and building/managing CI/CD pipelines to streamline deployments.
Identity & Security: A solid grasp of cloud identity management, specifically Azure AD (Entra ID) and AWS IAM, ensuring a secure and compliant environment.
Scripting Mastery: Advanced skills in PowerShell, Python, or similar languages to automate complex workflows.
Cloud Networking: Experience managing virtual networks, connectivity, and cloud native security protocols.
Background: 5+ years of experience in large scale corporate environments and a degree qualification in IT or a related discipline.

Why Us?

We offer the perfect intersection of enterprise stability and technological ambition.

Innovation at Our Core: We aren't just maintaining old systems; we are actively modernizing. From AI integration to advanced cloud automation, you will have the autonomy to implement new technologies that solve real business challenges.
Stability & Impact: Work for an established, market leading Australian brand. You’ll enjoy the job security of a large scale organization while seeing the direct impact of your work on our daily operations.
Professional Growth: We value "forward thinkers." You will be part of a team that encourages staying ahead of the curve, offering you the platform to sharpen your skills in the latest cloud and DevOps frameworks.
Culture of Excellence: Join a driven, motivated team that moves fast but values accuracy. We believe in doing things right the first time through automation and smart engineering.

How to Apply

If you are a cloud enthusiast who is passionate about building the future of infrastructure, we want to hear from you.

Apply Now by submitting your resume via the link below.

> Please note: Only applicants with full Australian work rights will be considered.

Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

What You’ll Be Doing

 

The Data Manager - Engineering is responsible for leading the development, scalability, and operational excellence of our core data platform(s). This role will lead a ‘Shared-Services’ data team and drive best-in-class engineering practices. The role is responsible for ensuring data availability and integrity via the delivery of high-quality, well-modelled, performant and reusable data products. They will be responsible for ensuring our engineering practices are saleable and oversee the automation, and optimization of data engineering/modelling/testing processes.

 

·         Define and deliver the data engineering roadmap aligned to business and analytics needs, designing scalable batch and real-time ETL/ELT pipelines

·         Drive best practices across data governance, security, lineage, cataloguing, and data quality

·         Build and lead a high-performing Data Shared-Services function (Data Engineers, Modellers, Testers)

·         Partner with Data Science, Analytics, Commercial, Marketing Technology, Architecture, Security, Cloud, SRE and DataOps teams

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Data Engineer to join a leading wholesale and distribution business, supporting the delivery of high-quality data solutions that drive operational and commercial decision-making.

In this role, you will design, build, and optimise scalable data pipelines and models, transforming complex and high-volume datasets into reliable, business-ready insights. You’ll play a key role in advancing our modern data platform, with a strong focus on Medallion architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold layers) and cloud-based technologies.

This is an excellent opportunity to work in a fast-paced, data-driven environment where your expertise will directly impact supply chain efficiency, reporting accuracy, and business performance.

Data Architecture & Modelling

Design and maintain scalable data models for analytics and reporting

Develop and manage Bronze, Silver, and Gold data layers

Data Engineering & Pipelines

Build and optimise ETL/ELT pipelines from ERP, CRM, and operational systems

Transform complex and semi-structured data (including JSON) into structured formats

Ensure pipelines are efficient, reliable, and scalable

Data Quality & Performance

Optimise SQL queries and data transformations for performance

Support data validation and reconciliation processes

Platform & Migration

Enhance performance and scalability of the data environment

Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement

Partner with BI teams and business stakeholders to deliver data solutions

Translate business needs into technical requirements
